description: Native mobile playback & polish. The watch page uses the native iOS <video controls> player + playsinline; custom overlays (skip/double-tap) live on the INLINE surface only — native fullscreen already provides speed menu, PiP, fullscreen, scrubber and must NOT be re-implemented. IN SCOPE: (1) Media Session API — metadata + artwork=/thumbnail/:id so lock screen/Control Center shows thumbnail, title, channel; play/pause/seek handlers. (2) Embedded file metadata -> description: extend the ffprobe scan to pull embedded tags, store on db.metadata, render on the watch page additively UNDER the blurred file-path block. (3) Varying comment counts — deterministic per-item count (4–14) like getStarRating. (4) Press-and-hold-to-2x on mobile inline video, arbitrated with existing single-tap (native play/pause) + double-tap-seek via hold timing; not in native fullscreen. ATTEMPT w/ caveat: rotate-to-fullscreen (iOS gates programmatic fullscreen behind a user gesture — may be Android-only; portrait must not force-exit unless user tapped fullscreen). DROPPED: custom PiP button (native provides one). ROADMAP: background-audio-when-app-switched (OS-gated), mini-player, .srt subtitles. Significant QA/code-review pass required, with the native-vs-custom seam and gesture arbitration in the acceptance criteria.

history.note: Feature initiated via /kickoff. Prior feature avi-ux-refinement shipped as v1.1.0 and its exec plan moved from docs/exec-plans/active/ to docs/exec-plans/completed/. Tech-debt tracker left intact (Closed row #1 unbounded-cache remains).

Bootstrapped and staged for discovery. No other overlapping active exec plans (only the avi-ux-refinement tombstone remains in active/). Tech-debt tracker Active table empty; Closed row from prior feature untouched. Description captured verbatim. Awaiting /prep-pm-discover to route product-manager.

Product-manager wrote exec plan docs/exec-plans/active/mobile-playback-polish.md covering: Media Session metadata/handlers, embedded-tag-to-description (pure parse fn + additive render under file-path block), deterministic 4-14 comment count, hold-to-2x gesture arbitration, best-effort rotate-to-fullscreen. PiP button explicitly dropped (native provides it). Testability plan splits pure/unit-testable logic (comment count, tag parser) from manual-only device/browser verification (Media Session, gestures, rotate, native coexistence). Grounded in public/watch.html, public/js/watch.js, public/js/common.js, server.js (extractMetadataAndThumbnail, /thumbnail/:id, db.metadata shape). No conflicts with ARCHITECTURE.md; no active tech-debt items touched. QA/code-review gate flagged. Ready for engineering-manager review -> principal-engineer design.

Principal-engineer appended ## Technical Design to docs/exec-plans/active/mobile-playback-polish.md and set artifacts.design. Design grounded in read of watch.js (setupSkipControls/populateMetadata/loadComments/skip/SKIP_SECONDS), common.js (getStarRating + module.exports shim), watch.html (video element, description-paragraph, player-wrapper), server.js (extractMetadataAndThumbnail exec, /api/videos/:id spreads ...item so tags auto-pass to client). Key decisions: (1) hold-to-2x EXTENDS the existing single touchend handler with touchstart/move/cancel + a state machine (HOLD_MS=350, MOVE_TOL=10, inNativeFullscreen()/isTouchDevice() guards) so tap/double-tap/hold never cross-fire; (2) setupMediaSession() in populateMetadata reuses skip()/SKIP_SECONDS, feature-detected, two same-URL artwork sizes; (3) parseFfprobeTags(input) pure+exported, whitelists title/comment/description/date, case-insensitive, dedups description==comment, {} on malformed; ffprobe cmd extended to -show_entries format=duration:format_tags, tags stored on db.metadata (no API change); (4) getCommentCount(id, poolSize) pure in common.js, 4-14, clamped, exported; (5) rotate-to-fullscreen orientation handler is portrait-no-op + try/catch. Two new unit tests specified (comment-count, ffprobe-tags). 7-step ordered task breakdown seeded (pure/testable first). Ready for engineering-manager task breakdown.
